channel.title: SSH on yexca'Blog
channel.description: Recent content in SSH on yexca'Blog

item.title: SSH Public Key Authentication
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview Introduction Typing a password every time I connect to a machine is a hassle (mostly because I keep forgetting it). One-Click Setup If your computer doesn’t have a public key yet, you need to generate one. It’s usually located at ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub. 1 ssh-keygen Then, run the following command on your local machine: 1 ssh-copy-id username@RemoteIP Replace username with the account you want to log into, and RemoteIP with …
